items.title: Oaktree Capital-Backed ITG Raises $312.2 Million in US IPO
items.description: Digital infrastructure services firm ITG Inc. raised $312.2 million in a US initial public offering, pricing its shares below a marketed range.

items.title: Shutterstock Shares Fall After Getty Says It Will End Merger
items.description: Shares of Shutterstock Inc. plunged about 30% in after-hours trading after Getty Images Holdings Inc. said it would cancel a planned merger due to a ruling from UK regulators.

items.title: Asian Stocks Set to Rise After Stellar Quarter: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian equities were poised to rise Wednesday after capping their best quarter in nearly 17 years, as a rally in chipmakers and signs of US economic resilience fueled optimism about corporate earnings.

items.title: Oil Steadies as Peace Talks Continue, Hormuz Traffic Recovers
items.description: Oil steadied after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Nike Appoints Pfizer CFO as New Finance Chief Amid Industry Experience Questions

items.title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal

items.title: Colombia’s Next President Taps Gómez as Incoming Finance Chief
items.description: Colombian President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella named Miguel Gómez to be his finance minister, tapping the former lawmaker from a conservative political dynasty to curb a rising national debt load

items.title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
items.description: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure.

items.title: Yao Ming on NBA Legacy, Steph Curry, Global Expansion
items.description: NBA Hall of Famer Yao Ming discusses being a part of the New York–Shanghai Science & Technology Forum. He discusses the global expansion of the NBA, and the popularity of basketball around the world.

items.title: Debt and Risky Bets Fuel South Korea and Taiwan's AI Stock Boom
items.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, how an AI-driven stock boom in Taiwan and South Korea is enticing investors to take big risks on one of the world’s hottest market rallies.

items.title: iHerb Is Said to Select Banks for $500 Million IPO This Year
items.description: iHerb, an online retailer focused on vitamins and supplements, has picked banks to lead its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Kura Sushi to Accelerate US Growth to Fend Off Rivals’ Expansion
items.description: Conveyor-belt sushi operator Kura Sushi Inc. has an ambitious target to grow its store count by 20% a year in the US, betting that it can build market share before a wave of competitors rolls in.
